title: Multi-Insurer Integration Pattern
description: Enforces Strategy + Factory pattern for insurer integrations. InsurerRouter auto-discovers adapters via Spring DI. New insurers require only a new adapter class and DB config row — zero changes to handlers or router.

book-references: Head First Design Patterns Ch. 1 — Strategy Pattern
Head First Design Patterns Ch. 4 — Factory Pattern
Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 9 — Service Discovery & Routing
Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 10 — Interaction Redundancy

nodes.name: Insurer Router (Factory)
nodes.description: Factory that resolves the correct InsurerPort adapter at runtime based on database configuration. Auto-discovers all InsurerPort Spring beans and indexes by adapterType.

nodes.name: InsurerPort Interface
nodes.description: Strategy interface defining submitRealTime(), submitBatch(), getCapabilities(), getAdapterType(). Domain-defined port — lives in domain layer.

nodes.name: Insurer Adapter (Concrete Strategy)
nodes.description: Technology-specific implementation of InsurerPort. Each adapter encapsulates protocol translation, error handling, and resilience annotations.

nodes.name: External Insurer API
nodes.description: Third-party insurer system with unique API protocol (REST/JSON, SOAP/XML, CSV/SFTP).

relationships.description: Router resolves correct adapter via InsurerRegistry config lookup + adapterType map
Adapter calls external insurer API with @CircuitBreaker + @Retry annotations

constraints.description: Never use if/else or switch on insurer type in handlers or controllers. Always resolve via InsurerRouter.
Adding a new insurer requires only: (1) new adapter class implementing InsurerPort, (2) Flyway migration for insurer_configurations row. Zero changes to handlers, domain, or router.
Every adapter method calling an external API must have @CircuitBreaker + @Retry annotations with named instances.
Every @CircuitBreaker-annotated method must have a typed fallback method returning graceful degradation.
InsurerRegistry.updateConfiguration() must use @CacheEvict. All config update paths route through this method.
